title: Extractor manifest — recipe for converting raw output files into a layer payload
description: JSON entry the server uses to convert raw outputs (TSV, Excel, RDS, VCF, BED, custom JSON, …) into a layer payload that gets wrapped in layer_envelope.schema.json. Declared in <atlas>/registries/data/extractors.registry.json and picked by the dispatcher based on the action's expected_outputs[*].layer_type. The parser is a Python function with signature parse(input_files: dict[str, str], params: dict) -> dict. Kept intentionally small per PIPELINE_FLOW.md §'Minimal stable core'.

layer_type.description: Layer type tag this extractor produces (e.g. 'fst_windows', 'inversion_candidate'). Matches action_manifest.expected_outputs[*].layer_type.

schema_version.description: Output schema (e.g. 'fst_windows_v1' for normalized, 'staging_inversion_candidate_v0' for staging). The dispatcher validates the parser's return against <atlas>/registries/schemas/schema_out/<schema_version>.schema.json.

stage.description: Which envelope stage the parser output belongs to.

parser.description: Python identifier 'module.path.function_name'. Function signature: parse(input_files, params) -> dict.

input_files.description: Default input file slot map; the dispatcher may override per-action. Keys are runner-known names, values are paths or {action_workdir}-relative templates.
